10.content: Thank you for being a part of the IPMAT Resources community! We're all here to support you in your journey to success.

15.content: Hello! I am sprakex, I was a non-math full dropper IPMAT aspirant in 2026, following are my scores/results for the various examinations which I had applied for.

29.content: My scores last year for IPMAT Indore and JIPMAT were 32/19/122 (177) and 230 respectively.

30.content: This website was first created in June 2024 as a place to host the freely available resources that aspiring IPMAT candidates might find useful. Since then, I haven't updated it much, but now that I have nothing better to do, I've decided to give the site a complete revamp—for better or for worse.

31.content: I have also decided to upload my own notes that I took throughout my drop year to this website in text format, but since not all of my notes were typed out, some of the notes were converted after I decided to make them public.

32.content: And of course, these are my personal notes and were taken from the perspective of a non-math student, so bear with me if some of them appear to be very basic.

59.content: Free mocks are hard to find. Most sites give you one or two for free and then push their paid courses. Here's a list of places where you can actually take full-length mocks without paying.

105.content: Most of these require a phone number or email signup. It's annoying but standard.

106.content: Don't just spam mocks. Take one, analyze where you're weak, work on those topics, then take another.

107.content: Start giving mocks at least 2-3 months before the exam. Early on, focus on sectionals rather than full-length.
